name: Ganas Ya - Jacksboro
description_short: GanasYa is a vehicle loan servicing platform that processes payments for auto loans originally sold through Tricolor Auto Sales, now managed by Vervent following Tricolor's bankruptcy.
description_long:
GanasYa operates as a loan servicing intermediary for customers who purchased vehicles through Tricolor Auto Sales. The company was established to manage customer accounts after Tricolor filed for bankruptcy in 2024. GanasYa continues to service loans under multiple brand names: Tricolor, Ganas, GanasYa, and Apoyo Financial, providing a unified platform for existing customers to manage their obligations.

GanasYa's primary function is payment processing and account management for auto loans. Customers can make payments through multiple channels including PayNearMe, online payment portals, and phone-based assistance. The platform provides balance inquiries, payment due date information, and text message reminders. Customer support is available via phone (888-448-7426) and email, with extended Saturday hours and Spanish-language agents available.

What distinguishes GanasYa is its transparent communication about the bankruptcy transition and its emphasis on fraud prevention. The company explicitly clarifies that loan obligations remain unchanged, interest rates do not increase, and payments continue to be applied to the original financing bank rather than Tricolor. The platform provides detailed guidance on legitimate payment methods and warns customers about common scams targeting borrowers in this situation.

GanasYa is primarily a loan servicing platform rather than a lender or credit repair company. It lacks the financial advisory, credit counseling, or debt resolution services typical of consumer finance companies. The platform's utility is limited to customers with existing Tricolor-branded auto loans and does not serve new borrowers or customers seeking credit improvement services.
pros: Multiple payment methods available including PayNearMe and online portals
No changes to loan terms, interest rates, or monthly payment amounts during servicing transition
Clear, transparent communication about bankruptcy implications and loan status
Fraud prevention guidance to protect customers from scams targeting borrowers in bankruptcy situations
Extended Saturday hours and Spanish-language customer support available
Automatic text message payment reminders sent to customers
Direct access to account balance and payment due dates through online portal
cons: Limited to existing Tricolor/Ganas/GanasYa/Apoyo auto loan customers only—cannot help new borrowers
No credit repair, debt counseling, or financial hardship resolution programs offered
Company is managing loans only because original lender (Tricolor) filed bankruptcy, indicating reputational and service continuity concerns
No proactive financial hardship programs documented; customers must self-initiate contact
Account escalation and potential default consequences remain in effect despite parent company bankruptcy
best_for: Existing Tricolor/Ganas/GanasYa/Apoyo auto loan customers needing payment management and account access
Borrowers seeking clarity on their loan status and obligations following Tricolor's bankruptcy
Spanish-speaking customers requiring bilingual customer support
diagnosis: GanasYa is exclusively for existing auto loan customers formerly serviced by Tricolor Auto Sales who need to manage payments and account information post-bankruptcy. The platform is a loan servicer, not a lender or credit repair company, and provides no credit improvement, debt resolution, or financial advisory services. Consumers seeking credit repair, debt relief, or new financing should not contact this company.
services: Auto loan payment processing through PayNearMe
Online payment portal and account management
Account balance inquiry and payment due date tracking
Financial hardship consultation (available upon customer request)
Warranty and repair coverage inquiry assistance
